headline: Putrajaya spent RM890 million on consultants in 2025 alone
context: Federal government spending on external consultants reached RM890 million in 2025, a 35% increase from 2023, despite having 1.7 million civil servants on payroll.

cards.big: RM890 million on consultants — while 1.7 million civil servants already on payroll
cards.sub: Government outsourcing expertise it should have built internally over decades

cards.big: Treasury Circular 2/2015 caps consulting fees but exempts 'strategic projects'
cards.sub: Strategic exemption applied to 62% of consulting contracts in 2025

cards.big: Top 5 consulting firms received RM340 million — 38% of total consulting spend
cards.sub: Concentration of contracts suggests relationship-based rather than competitive selection

cards.big: Consulting reports from 2020-2023 had only 29% implementation rate
cards.sub: Government pays for advice it doesn't follow — RM630 million in unused reports

cards.big: A government that cannot use its own expertise is a government that has failed at capacity building
Should consulting contracts require mandatory implementation plans before approval?
